name: Llama 3.1 8B
notes: Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from bartowski HF repo. Default ollama tag pulls 4.9GB Q4_K_M. Min RAM estimate includes model + small KV cache overhead.
license_note: Llama 3.1 Community License: free under 700M MAU.

name: Llama 3.3 70B
notes: Released December 6, 2024. Delivers near-405B performance at 70B cost. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from bartowski HF repo cross-validated against Ollama tags page (43GB and 75GB displayed).
license_note: Llama 3.3 Community License: free under 700M MAU.

name: Llama 3.2 3B
notes: Released September 25, 2024. Text-only edge model. Ollama default tag is 3B at 2.0GB. HF unsloth repo confirms 2.02GB Q4_K_M and 3.42GB Q8_0.
license_note: Llama 3.2 Community License: free under 700M MAU.

name: Llama 3.2 1B
notes: Released September 25, 2024. Smallest Llama model for edge/on-device use. Ollama tag shows 1.3GB (Q8_0 equivalent default). HF unsloth confirms Q4_K_M at 808MB (~0.81GB) and Q8_0 at 1.32GB.
license_note: Llama 3.2 Community License: free under 700M MAU.

name: Mistral 7B
notes: Latest is v0.3 (released May 2024), adds function calling and extended vocabulary. Ollama default tag shows 4.4GB; bartowski HF repo gives precise 4.37GB Q4_K_M and 7.70GB Q8_0 for v0.3.

name: Mistral Small 3 24B
notes: Mistral Small 3.2 released June 20, 2025. Latest in the Mistral Small 3 line. Context 128K confirmed by official Mistral docs. Q4_K_M=14.33GB and Q8_0=25.05GB from bartowski HF repo (mistralai_Mistral-Small-3.2-24B-Instruct-2506-GGUF). Ollama page shows 15GB for the default tag.

name: Phi-4 14B
notes: Released December 12, 2024 by Microsoft. Strong math and reasoning. Default context 16K. Ollama shows 9.1GB; bartowski HF gives precise 9.05GB Q4_K_M and 15.58GB Q8_0.

name: Phi-4-mini 3.8B
notes: Released February 2025 by Microsoft. Dense decoder-only transformer with 200K vocabulary. 128K context. Q4_K_M=2.49GB and Q8_0=4.08GB from bartowski HF repo (microsoft_Phi-4-mini-instruct-GGUF). Requires Ollama 0.5.13+.

name: Gemma 2 9B
notes: Released June 27, 2024. Uses sliding window attention with 8K context. Ollama default tag shows 5.4GB; bartowski HF repo gives precise 5.76GB Q4_K_M and 9.83GB Q8_0.
license_note: Gemma Terms of Use apply to commercial use.

name: Gemma 2 27B
notes: Released June 27, 2024. Uses sliding window attention with 8K context. Ollama default tag shows 16GB; bartowski HF repo gives precise 16.65GB Q4_K_M and 28.94GB Q8_0.
license_note: Gemma Terms of Use apply to commercial use.

name: Gemma 3 4B
notes: Released March 12, 2025. Multimodal (text + image), 140+ languages. Ollama shows 3.3GB default; bartowski HF (google_gemma-3-4b-it-GGUF) gives precise 2.49GB Q4_K_M and 4.13GB Q8_0.
license_note: Gemma Terms of Use apply to commercial use.

name: Gemma 3 12B
notes: Released March 12, 2025. Multimodal (text + image), 140+ languages. Ollama shows 8.1GB default; bartowski HF (google_gemma-3-12b-it-GGUF) gives precise 7.30GB Q4_K_M and 12.51GB Q8_0.
license_note: Gemma Terms of Use apply to commercial use.

name: Gemma 3 27B
notes: Released March 12, 2025. Multimodal (text + image), 140+ languages. Ollama shows 17GB default; bartowski HF (google_gemma-3-27b-it-GGUF) gives precise 16.55GB Q4_K_M and 28.71GB Q8_0.
license_note: Gemma Terms of Use apply to commercial use.

name: Qwen2.5 7B
notes: Ollama shows 4.7GB (rounded); bartowski HF repo gives 4.68GB Q4_K_M and 8.10GB Q8_0. Default context 128K per official Qwen blog. Min RAM estimate adds ~1-2GB overhead over Q4_K_M size.

name: Qwen2.5 14B
notes: Ollama shows 9.0GB (rounded); bartowski HF repo gives 8.99GB Q4_K_M and 15.70GB Q8_0. Default context 128K.

name: Qwen2.5 32B
notes: Ollama shows 20GB (rounded); bartowski HF repo gives 19.85GB Q4_K_M and 34.82GB Q8_0. Default context 128K.

name: Qwen2.5 72B
notes: Ollama shows 47GB (rounded); bartowski HF repo gives 47.42GB Q4_K_M and 77.26GB Q8_0. Default context 128K. Requires multi-GPU or high-VRAM single GPU setup.
license_note: Qwen License: free for commercial use under 100M monthly active users.

name: Qwen3 8B
notes: Official Qwen/Qwen3-8B-GGUF HF repo lists Q4_K_M=5.03GB, Q8_0=8.71GB. Ollama library shows 5.2GB (rounded). Qwen3 released 2025-04-29. Context 32K native, extendable to 128K via YaRN.

name: Qwen3 14B
notes: Official Qwen/Qwen3-14B-GGUF HF repo lists Q4_K_M=9GB, Q8_0=15.7GB. Confirmed by Ollama tags page (9.3GB rounded) and search cross-check. Context 32K native, 128K via YaRN.

name: Qwen3 32B
notes: Official Qwen/Qwen3-32B-GGUF HF repo lists Q4_K_M=19.8GB, Q8_0=34.8GB. Cross-checked with Ollama tags page (20GB rounded). Context 32K native, 128K via YaRN.

name: Qwen3 30B-A3B
notes: MoE: 30.5B total / 3.3B active (128 total experts, 8 activated per token). Q4_K_M=18.6GB and Q8_0=32.5GB from official unsloth/Qwen3-30B-A3B-GGUF HF repo, cross-confirmed with Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B-GGUF. Native context 32K, extendable to 128K via YaRN. Despite large Q4 size, inference is fast due to only 3.3B active params.

name: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen 7B
notes: Distilled from DeepSeek-R1 onto Qwen2.5-7B base. Released 2025-01-20. Q4_K_M=4.68GB, Q8_0=8.10GB from bartowski HF repo. Ollama shows 4.7GB. Context 128K.

name: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen 14B
notes: Distilled from DeepSeek-R1 onto Qwen2.5-14B base. Released 2025-01-20. Q4_K_M=8.99GB, Q8_0=15.70GB from bartowski HF repo. Ollama shows 9.0GB. Context 128K.

name: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama 8B
notes: Distilled from DeepSeek-R1 onto Llama-3.1-8B base. Released 2025-01-20. Q4_K_M=4.92GB, Q8_0=8.54GB from bartowski HF repo. Ollama shows 5.2GB for the 8b tag. Context 128K.

name: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen 32B
notes: Distilled from DeepSeek-R1 onto Qwen2.5-32B base. Released 2025-01-20. Q4_K_M=19.85GB, Q8_0=34.82GB from bartowski HF repo. Ollama shows 20GB. Context 128K.

name: DeepSeek-V2-Lite
notes: MoE: 16B total / 2.4B active (2 shared + 64 routed experts per layer, 6 activated per token). Released 2024-05-16. Q4_K_M=10.4GB, Q8_0=16.8GB from mradermacher HF GGUF repo (bartowski does not host this model). Ollama deepseek-v2:16b=8.9GB uses different quantization. Context 32K native. Despite 16B total params, inference speed is close to 2.4B dense models.
license_note: DeepSeek Model License permits commercial use.

name: SmolLM2 1.7B
notes: Released by HuggingFace 2024-11-01. Q4_K_M=1.06GB, Q8_0=1.82GB from bartowski/SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct-GGUF HF repo, cross-confirmed with HuggingFaceTB official GGUF repo. Ollama shows 1.8GB. Context 8K. Efficient edge/on-device model.

name: Qwen2.5 0.5B
notes: Smallest Qwen2.5 dense model. Trained on 18T tokens, supports 29+ languages, 128K context (generates up to 8K). Ideal for on-device and microcontroller-class edge inference.

name: Qwen2.5 1.5B
notes: Strong 1.5B edge model from Alibaba. 128K context, 8K generation. Popular for phone and laptop inference with good multilingual and instruction-following capability.

name: Qwen2.5 3B
notes: Mid-range Qwen2.5 dense model. 128K context window, good coding and math performance relative to size. Runs on most 8GB RAM devices at Q4.
license_note: Qwen Research License: non-commercial use only.

name: Qwen3 0.6B
notes: Smallest Qwen3 dense model. Supports thinking/non-thinking dual mode, 32K native context (extendable to 128K via YaRN). Designed for extreme edge and on-device use.

name: Qwen3 1.7B
notes: Dual-mode (thinking/non-thinking) 1.7B dense model. 32K native context (YaRN to 128K). Strong instruction-following and tool-use for its class.

name: Qwen3 4B
notes: Claimed to rival Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct on several benchmarks. 32K native context (YaRN to 128K). Dual thinking/non-thinking mode. Excellent laptop-class edge model.

name: Gemma 2 2B
notes: Google's smallest Gemma 2 model. Uses sliding-window attention (local 4K + global 8K). Strong code and reasoning for its size. Released July 31, 2024 separately from the 9B/27B launch.
license_note: Gemma Terms of Use apply to commercial use.

name: Gemma 3 1B
notes: Google's ultra-small Gemma 3 model. 32K context, multilingual support, strong reasoning for 1B scale. Designed to run on a single GPU or phone-class hardware.
license_note: Gemma Terms of Use apply to commercial use.

name: SmolLM2 135M
notes: Hugging Face's smallest SLM. 135M params, fits in ~105MB at Q4_K_M. Designed for microcontrollers and on-device inference. Trained on 2T tokens (FineWeb-Edu, DCLM, The Stack).

name: SmolLM2 360M
notes: Mid-tier SmolLM2. 362M params, 271MB at Q4_K_M. Trained on 4T tokens. Suitable for phones and embedded Linux devices. Apache 2.0 license.

name: TinyLlama 1.1B
notes: Community classic 1.1B model pretrained on 3T tokens using Llama-2 architecture. 2048 token context. Very small GGUF footprint (669MB Q4_K_M). Good baseline for edge benchmarking.

name: Granite 3.1 2B
notes: IBM's enterprise-grade 2B dense SLM. 128K context (extended from 4K via progressive RoPE training on ~500B tokens). Optimized for tool-use and RAG workloads. Apache 2.0.

name: Phi-3.5-mini 3.8B
notes: Microsoft's 3.8B long-context SLM. 128K context, multilingual (20+ languages). Trained on 3.4T tokens. Strong performance vs similarly-sized models on reasoning and document QA.

name: Sarvam-M 24B
notes: Dense 24B fine-tuned from Mistral-Small-3.1-24B-Base. Hybrid thinking mode. Q4_K_M 14.3GB and Q8_0 25.1GB confirmed from two independent GGUF repos (lmstudio-community, Mungert) plus the official sarvamai Q8 repo. Context 32K from config.json.

name: Sarvam-1 2B
notes: 2B dense model trained from scratch, optimized for 10 Indic languages plus English. Released 2024-10-24 under the Sarvam non-commercial license (not Apache). GGUF sizes from bartowski: Q4_K_M 1.55GB, Q8_0 2.69GB. Context 8K from the model card.
license_note: Non-commercial use only per the Sarvam license. Check the current HuggingFace model card for updates.

name: Sarvam-30B
notes: MoE with 128 sparse experts, top-6 routing, 2.4B active params. Released 2026-03 under Apache 2.0. Q4_K_M 19.6GB confirmed by summing the 6 shards in the official GGUF repo. No official Q8_0 GGUF released.

name: Sarvam-105B
notes: MoE with 128 experts, top-8 routing plus one shared expert, 10.3B active params. Released 2026-03 under Apache 2.0. Q4_K_M 64.2GB confirmed by summing the 9 shards in the official GGUF repo. Server-class; no Q8_0 GGUF exists.

name: Qwen2.5 Coder 0.5B
notes: Qwen2.5-Coder, code-specialized small model. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the bartowski GGUF repo. 32K context.

name: Qwen2.5 Coder 1.5B
notes: Qwen2.5-Coder, code-specialized. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the bartowski GGUF repo. 32K context.

name: Qwen2.5 Coder 3B
notes: Qwen2.5-Coder, code-specialized. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the bartowski GGUF repo. 32K context. Qwen Research License restricts commercial use.
license_note: Qwen Research License: non-commercial use only.

name: Qwen2.5 Coder 7B
notes: Qwen2.5-Coder, code-specialized. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the bartowski GGUF repo. 32K native context, 128K with YaRN.

name: Qwen2.5 Coder 14B
notes: Qwen2.5-Coder, code-specialized. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the bartowski GGUF repo. 32K native context, 128K with YaRN.

name: Qwen2.5 Coder 32B
notes: Qwen2.5-Coder, code-specialized. Matches frontier coding quality at 32B. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the bartowski GGUF repo. 32K native context, 128K with YaRN.

name: Mistral Nemo 12B
notes: Mistral Nemo 12B, built with NVIDIA, 128K context. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the bartowski GGUF repo.

name: Mixtral 8x7B
notes: Mixtral 8x7B sparse MoE: 46.7B total, 12.9B active (2 of 8 experts per token). All experts must fit in memory. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the MaziyarPanahi GGUF repo.

name: DeepSeek R1
notes: DeepSeek R1, 671B sparse MoE (37B active). Q4_K_M is ~377GB across a 9-file series, beyond any consumer device. unsloth 1.58-bit dynamic quants run in ~140-160GB, so a 192GB Mac Studio can run it slowly. Size from the unsloth GGUF repo, cross-checked against the Ollama 671B tag.

name: DeepSeek V3
notes: DeepSeek V3 (0324 build), 671B sparse MoE (37B active). Q4_K_M is ~377GB across a 9-file series. Low-bit dynamic quants fit ~150GB. Size from the unsloth GGUF repo.

name: Qwen3 235B A22B
notes: Qwen3 235B sparse MoE (22B active). Q4_K_M ~132GB, fits a 192GB Mac. Size from the unsloth GGUF repo, exact-matched to the Ollama 235B tag.

name: Llama 4 Scout
notes: Llama 4 Scout, 109B MoE (17B active), natively multimodal with very long context. Q4_K_M ~61GB across a 2-file series. Size from the unsloth GGUF repo (Ollama llama4:scout is a comparable ~63GB build).
license_note: Llama 4 Community License: free under 700M MAU, with use restrictions.

name: gpt-oss 20B
notes: OpenAI gpt-oss 20B, MoE (3.6B active). Ships native MXFP4 4-bit; the official ggml-org GGUF is ~11.3GB (the real default, not a Q4_K_M requant). Runs on a 16GB GPU, or a Mac with 24GB+ of unified memory for working headroom.

name: gpt-oss 120B
notes: OpenAI gpt-oss 120B, MoE (5.1B active). Native MXFP4; the official ggml-org GGUF is ~59GB, so it fits an 80GB GPU or a 96GB+ Mac.

name: Yi 1.5 34B
notes: Yi 1.5 34B from 01.AI. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the bartowski GGUF repo.

name: Command R 35B
notes: Cohere Command R 35B, tuned for RAG and tool use, 128K context. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the bartowski GGUF repo.
license_note: CC BY-NC 4.0: non-commercial use only.

name: GLM-4 9B
notes: Zhipu GLM-4 9B, multilingual, 128K context. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the bartowski GGUF repo.
license_note: GLM-4 Model License: review terms for commercial use.

name: Falcon3 10B
notes: TII Falcon3 10B. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the bartowski GGUF repo, exact-matched to the Ollama falcon3:10b tag.

name: Granite 4.0 H Small
notes: IBM Granite 4.0 H Small, hybrid Mamba/transformer MoE (32B total, 9B active), 128K context. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the unsloth GGUF repo, matched to the Ollama granite4:small-h tag.

name: SmolLM3 3B
notes: Hugging Face SmolLM3 3B, long-context small model. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sizes from the unsloth GGUF repo.

name: Qwen2.5-VL 3B
notes: Qwen2.5-VL 3B vision-language model: chats about images. Q4_K_M ~1.8GB plus a ~1.25GB fp16 vision projector (mmproj), folded into the totals. Sizes from the ggml-org GGUF repo. Vision input needs an mmproj-capable runtime (llama.cpp, LM Studio); many mobile runtimes do not support it yet.

name: Qwen2.5-VL 7B
notes: Qwen2.5-VL 7B vision-language model: chats about images. Q4_K_M ~4.4GB plus a ~1.26GB fp16 vision projector (mmproj), folded into the totals. Sizes from the ggml-org GGUF repo. Vision needs an mmproj-capable runtime; many mobile runtimes do not support it yet.

name: Llama 3.2 Vision 11B
notes: Meta Llama 3.2 Vision 11B: image understanding plus text. Q4 ~5.55GB plus a ~1.81GB vision projector, folded into the totals (matches the ~7.3GB Ollama llama3.2-vision tag). Vision needs an mmproj-capable runtime.
license_note: Llama 3.2 Community License: free under 700M MAU.

name: GLM-4.5-Air
notes: MoE: 128 routed experts + 1 shared expert, activates 9 per token. 106B total / 12B active per zai-org HF card and artificialanalysis.ai. Context 128K (max_position_embeddings=131072) per search results and llm-stats. MIT license, commercial use explicitly permitted per zai-org HF card. Released July 28 2025 per artificialanalysis.ai and llm-stats. Q4_K_M 73.50 GB and Q8_0 117.46 GB measured from bartowski GGUF file listing. fp16_gb not listed in bartowski repo (only goes to Q8_0 at 117.46 GB). No official ollama.com/library tag; community tag MichelRosselli/GLM-4.5-Air exists but is unofficial.

name: GLM-4-32B-0414
notes: Dense 32B transformer. Native context 32K (max_position_embeddings=32768 in config.json); a separate GLM-4-32B-0414-128K variant extends to 128K via YaRN rope scaling. Default_context_k set to 32 for the base model. MIT license per both THUDM and zai-org HF repos. Released April 14 2025 (confirmed via zai-org/GLM-4 GitHub). Q4_K_M 19.7 GB, Q8_0 34.6 GB, FP16 65.1 GB all measured from bartowski GGUF file listing. No official ollama.com/library tag; community tags exist (JollyLlama/GLM-4-32B-0414-Q4_K_M, sammcj/glm-4-32b-0414).

name: GLM-4-9B-0414
notes: Dense 9B transformer. Native context 32K (same config pattern as 32B sibling); YaRN extension reaches 128K. MIT license per THUDM and zai-org HF repos. Released April 14 2025 alongside GLM-4-32B-0414. Q4_K_M 6.17 GB, Q8_0 10.00 GB, FP16 18.81 GB all measured from bartowski GGUF file listing. No official ollama.com/library tag; community tag milkey/GLM-4-9B-0414 exists.

name: Gemma 3 270M
notes: Q4_K_M not present in ggml-org/gemma-3-270m-GGUF repo (only Q8_0 at 292 MB published there); fp16 from ollama gemma3:270m-it-fp16 at 543 MB. Gemma license requires acceptance; free for most uses but has content and redistribution conditions. 32K context confirmed from Ollama library listing and HF model card.
license_note: Gemma Terms of Use apply to commercial use.

name: LFM2 350M
notes: Exact params: 354,483,968. Hybrid architecture: 10 double-gated short-range LIV convolution blocks + 6 GQA blocks. Context 128K from config.json max_position_embeddings. LFM Open License: free for academic/research and companies with revenue under $10M; larger enterprises must license commercially. No official ollama.com/library tag; community sam860/LFM2:350m exists. Paper arXiv:2511.23404 released November 2025. Q4_K_M 229 MB is the standard (non-hip-optimized) file.
license_note: LFM Open License: free under $10M revenue; larger companies need a commercial license.

name: LFM2 700M
notes: Exact params: 742,489,344. Same hybrid conv+GQA architecture as other LFM2 variants. Context 128K from config.json. Commercial terms same as LFM2-350M. No official ollama.com/library tag. Q4_K_M 469 MB is the standard (non-hip-optimized) file.
license_note: LFM Open License: free under $10M revenue; larger companies need a commercial license.

name: LFM2 1.2B
notes: Exact params: 1,170,340,608. 16 total layers (10 conv + 6 GQA). Context 128K from config.json. Commercial terms same as other LFM2 models. No official ollama.com/library tag; LiquidAI only publishes LFM2.5 variants officially on Ollama. Q4_K_M 731 MB is the standard file (non-hip-optimized is 796 MB).
license_note: LFM Open License: free under $10M revenue; larger companies need a commercial license.

name: Apple OpenELM 1.1B
notes: Context 2,048 tokens confirmed from config.json max_context_length. Apple AMLR (Apple Machine Learning Research) license is research-only and prohibits commercial use. Layer-wise scaling transformer architecture, trained on ~1.8T tokens. No official Ollama library tag. Community GGUF from mradermacher; LiteLLMs/OpenELM-1_1B-GGUF also exists with same Q4_K_M size (673 MB). Paper published April 2024.
license_note: apple-amlr license; non-commercial / research use only.

name: Apple OpenELM 3B
notes: Context 2,048 tokens (same family architecture as 1.1B; 3B config has model_dim 3072). Apple AMLR license prohibits commercial use. Community GGUFs: mradermacher/OpenELM-3B-GGUF (base) and spongeman/OpenELM-3B-Instruct-Q4_K_M-GGUF (instruct, 1.89 GB) both confirm Q4_K_M at 1.89 GB. No official Ollama library tag; community tomasmcm/openelm exists.
license_note: apple-amlr license; non-commercial / research use only.

name: Phi-4-reasoning
notes: Microsoft's reasoning-tuned Phi-4. Strong math and logic for a 14B; runs on a 16GB machine at Q4_K_M.

name: Mistral Small 3.1 24B
notes: Mistral Small 3.1: multimodal with a 128K context, and the base both Magistral and Devstral build on.

name: Magistral Small
notes: Mistral's open reasoning model, built on Small 3.1 with reinforcement learning. Tool use and vision.

name: Devstral Small
notes: Mistral's coding-agent model, strong on SWE-bench. A community favorite for local Cline and OpenHands.

name: Gemma 3n E4B
notes: Google's on-device Gemma 3n (MatFormer): about 8B raw params but a ~4B effective memory footprint. Multimodal, built for phones and laptops.
license_note: Gemma Terms of Use apply to commercial use.

name: Llama 4 Maverick
notes: The larger Llama 4: 400B total / 17B active across 128 experts, 1M context. Needs server-class memory even at Q4.
license_note: Llama 4 Community License; commercial use allowed below Meta's user threshold.

name: DeepSeek-R1-0528
notes: The May 2025 update to DeepSeek-R1 (671B MoE, 37B active). Deeper reasoning; runs only on a multi-GPU rig or a large Mac cluster.

name: DeepSeek-R1-0528-Qwen3-8B
notes: The runnable distill of R1-0528 into Qwen3-8B. Brings the updated reasoning to a 16GB machine.

name: Qwen3-Coder 30B-A3B
notes: Qwen3's coding MoE: 30B total, ~3B active, 256K context. A local coding leader that runs on consumer hardware.

name: Llama-3.3-Nemotron-Super-49B-v1
notes: NVIDIA's reasoning-tuned Llama 3.3 derivative. 49B dense; fits a 32GB+ GPU or a high-memory Mac at Q4.
license: NVIDIA Open Model License + Llama 3.3 Community License
license_note: NVIDIA Open Model + Llama 3.3 Community License; check the terms for commercial use.

name: EXAONE 4.0 32B
notes: LG's 32B hybrid reasoning model, strong Korean and English. Non-commercial license.
license: EXAONE AI Model License Agreement 1.2 - NC
license_note: EXAONE AI Model License Agreement 1.2 - NC: non-commercial / research use only.

name: OLMo 2 32B Instruct
notes: AI2's fully open 32B (weights, training data and code all public). Note the small 4K context.

name: Granite 4.0 H Small
notes: IBM Granite 4.0: a hybrid Mamba-2 + Transformer MoE, 32B total / 9B active, 128K context. Apache-2.0.

name: Command A
notes: Cohere's 111B flagship with a 256K context, strong for RAG and tool use. Non-commercial license.
license_note: cc-by-nc-4.0: non-commercial use only.

name: GLM-4.6
notes: Z.ai's GLM-4.6 flagship: 357B MoE, 32B active, 200K context, MIT. Needs a multi-GPU rig or a large Mac cluster.

name: Qwen3-Coder 480B-A35B Instruct
notes: Qwen3's flagship coding MoE: 480B total / 35B active, 256K context. Server-class hardware; the 30B-A3B sibling runs on consumer machines.

name: Kimi K2 Instruct
notes: Moonshot's 1T-parameter MoE (32B active). One of the largest open models; needs a serious cluster even at Q4.
license_note: Modified MIT; commercial use allowed below a large-user threshold.

name: Hunyuan-A13B-Instruct
notes: Tencent's Hunyuan MoE: 80B total / 13B active, 256K context. Runs on a 64GB+ machine at Q4.
license_note: Tencent Hunyuan Community License; restricted in the EU and above 100M MAU.

name: Seed-OSS 36B Instruct
notes: ByteDance's open 36B with a 512K context. Apache-2.0.

name: ERNIE 4.5 21B-A3B
notes: Baidu's open ERNIE 4.5 MoE: 21B total / 3B active, runs on consumer hardware. Apache-2.0.

name: MiniMax-M1-80k
notes: MiniMax's 456B reasoning MoE (45.9B active) with a 1M context. Open weights, but no prebuilt Q4 GGUF yet, so the size shown is computed from the formula.

name: Nemotron Nano 9B v2
notes: NVIDIA's small hybrid Mamba-Transformer. 9B, runs on a 16GB machine.
license_note: NVIDIA Open Model License; check the terms for commercial use.

name: Phi-4-mini-reasoning
notes: Microsoft's tiny reasoning model: 3.8B, MIT, runs on almost anything.

name: Falcon-H1-34B-Instruct
notes: TII's hybrid Mamba-Transformer Falcon. 34B, 256K context.
license_note: Falcon LLM license; permissive but check the terms.

name: dots.llm1
notes: Rednote's open MoE: 142B total / 14B active, MIT.

name: Gemma 4 E2B
notes: Google's on-device Gemma 4 (PLE): 2.3B effective params, 5.1B raw with per-layer embeddings. Multimodal (vision, ~1GB mmproj extra). Q4_K_M/Q8_0/BF16 sizes from unsloth HF repo. The Ollama gemma4:e2b blob is 7.2GB because it bundles full-precision per-layer embeddings, same pattern as Gemma 3n. 128K context.

name: Gemma 4 E4B
notes: Gemma 4's larger on-device model (PLE): 4.5B effective params, 8B raw with per-layer embeddings. Multimodal (vision, ~1GB mmproj extra). Sizes from unsloth HF repo; the Ollama gemma4:e4b blob is 9.6GB with full-precision embeddings bundled, same pattern as Gemma 3n. 128K context. Ollama v0.31.1 (2026-06-30) roughly doubled Gemma 4 speed on Apple Silicon via MLX multi-token prediction.

name: Gemma 4 12B
notes: Mid-size dense Gemma 4, multimodal (vision, ~0.2GB mmproj extra). Sizes from unsloth HF repo; Ollama default tag pulls the 7.6GB Q4_K_M. 256K context. Gemma 4 ships under Apache-2.0, unlike Gemma 3's Gemma license.

name: Gemma 4 26B-A4B
notes: Google's first MoE Gemma: 25.2B total per the model card (26.5B in safetensors incl. vision tower), 3.8B active. Q4_K_M from bartowski, Q8_0/BF16 from unsloth. Multimodal. 256K context. Despite the 17GB Q4 file, inference speed tracks the 3.8B active params.

name: Gemma 4 31B
notes: Gemma 4 flagship dense model: 30.7B text params per the model card, 32.7B in safetensors incl. vision tower. Multimodal, 256K context. Sizes from unsloth HF repo; Ollama default pulls 20GB Q4_K_M. Successor slot to Gemma 3 27B.

name: Qwen3.6 27B
notes: Qwen3.6 dense flagship for single-GPU rigs: 27B dense, natively multimodal (vision, ~0.9GB mmproj extra), 256K context. Sizes from unsloth HF repo; Ollama default pulls 17GB Q4_K_M. Apache-2.0.

name: Qwen3.6 35B-A3B
notes: Successor to Qwen3 30B-A3B: 35B total, 3B active (256 experts, 8 active per token). Multimodal, 256K context. Q4_K_M from bartowski, Q8_0/BF16 from unsloth; Ollama default pulls 24GB. Fast on unified-memory machines thanks to the 3B active params.

name: Qwen-AgentWorld 35B-A3B
notes: Alibaba's agent 'world model' (June 2026) built on the Qwen3.6 35B-A3B MoE base: trained on 10M+ real environment trajectories across MCP, search, terminal, SWE, web, OS and Android domains. Vision-capable (mmproj required). Q4_K_M is unsloth's UD-Q4_K_M dynamic quant; Q8_0/BF16 are plain quants from the same repo. Not on Ollama's library; run via llama.cpp or LM Studio.

name: GLM-5.2
notes: Z.ai's flagship long-horizon model: 744B MoE, 40B active, DeepSeek Sparse Attention, 1M context, MIT. Quant sums from unsloth's split GGUFs (Q4_K_M and Q8_0 are UD dynamic quants). Even the 2-bit UD-Q2_K_XL is 254GB, beyond any single consumer machine; Ollama lists it as cloud-only. Multi-node or big-cluster territory.

name: Laguna XS 2.1
notes: poolside's open-weight coding MoE: 33B total, 3B active (256 experts, 8 per token), 256K context, tuned for SWE-bench-style agentic work. Sizes from the Ollama tag listing. The 2.1 release relicensed from Apache-2.0 (XS.2) to OpenMDW-1.1, the Linux Foundation's permissive open-weights license.

name: Ornith 1.0 9B
notes: DeepReinforce's coding-agent model, dense 9B, post-trained for state-of-the-art agentic coding at its size. 256K context, MIT. Sizes from the Ollama tag listing, matching the vendor GGUF repo.

name: Ornith 1.0 35B
notes: DeepReinforce's larger coding-agent MoE: 35B total, 3B active (built on the Qwen3.6 35B-A3B base), 256K context, MIT. Fast on unified memory thanks to the 3B active params.

name: North Mini Code 1.0
notes: Cohere Labs' open-weight code model: 30B total, 3B active, optimized for code generation, agentic software engineering and terminal tasks. Unusually long 500K context. Apache-2.0. Sizes from the Ollama tag listing.

name: LFM2.5 8B-A1B
notes: Liquid AI's reasoning-tuned MoE for on-device use: 8.3B total, 1.5B active, 128K context. Despite the A1B name the vendor card states 1.5B active params. Sizes from the Ollama tag listing, matching the vendor GGUF repo.
license_note: LFM Open License: free under $10M revenue; larger companies need a commercial license.

name: LFM2.5 1.2B
notes: The LFM2 successor at phone size: dense 1.17B, 128K context. Sizes measured from the vendor GGUF repo. Runs on any recent phone; the LFM2 sibling entries cover the older 350M-1.2B line.
license_note: LFM Open License: free under $10M revenue; larger companies need a commercial license.

name: Granite 4.1 3B
notes: IBM's Granite 4.1 small: dense 3.4B, 128K context, Apache-2.0. Enterprise-leaning instruction model; sizes from the Ollama tag listing.

name: Granite 4.1 8B
notes: The mid Granite 4.1: dense 8.8B, 128K context, Apache-2.0. The most-downloaded of the 4.1 line; comfortable on a 16GB machine at Q4.

name: Granite 4.1 30B
notes: Granite 4.1 flagship: dense 28.9B, 128K context, Apache-2.0. Needs a 32GB-class machine at Q4.

name: MiniCPM-V 4.6
notes: OpenBMB's edge vision-language model: SigLIP2-400M vision tower on a Qwen3.5-0.8B LLM, ~1.3B total, 256K context. Text GGUF sizes from the vendor repo; vision needs the ~1.1GB f16 mmproj on top (the 1.6GB Ollama blob bundles it). Image, multi-image and video understanding on phone-class hardware.

name: Kimi K2.6
notes: Moonshot's K2 successor: 1T total, 32B active (384 experts), now vision-capable, 256K context. Q4 figure is unsloth's UD-Q4_K_XL dynamic quant (583.71GB, split GGUFs); even the 2-bit UD-Q2_K_XL is 340GB. Ollama lists it as cloud-only; multi-node territory locally.
license_note: Modified MIT; commercial use allowed below a large-user threshold, attribution above it.

name: Kimi K2.7 Code
notes: Moonshot's coding variant of the 1T K2 line: 32B active, vision-capable, 256K context. Q4 figure is unsloth's UD-Q4_K_XL (583.71GB); the 1-bit UD-IQ1_M floor is still 304GB. Cloud-only on Ollama; no single consumer machine runs it.
license_note: Modified MIT; commercial use allowed below a large-user threshold, attribution above it.

name: DeepSeek-V4-Flash
notes: DeepSeek's efficient V4: 284B total, 13B active, 1M context, MIT. Q4_K_M and Q8_0 sums from split community GGUFs; bartowski's MXFP4 build is 156GB. The 1-bit floor (~62GB) fits a 96GB unified-memory machine, barely.

name: DeepSeek-V4-Pro
notes: The largest open-weight model tracked here: 1.6T total, 49B active, 1M context, MIT. Q4 is ~955GB (split community GGUFs); even 2-bit is 574GB. Strictly multi-node; listed for the record and the licence, not for a shopping decision.

name: MiniMax M3
notes: MiniMax's natively multimodal flagship: 428B total, 23B active, 1M context. Q4 figure is unsloth's UD-Q4_K_M (264GB); the 2-bit UD-Q2_K_XL at 143GB is the realistic single-big-rig floor. Cloud-only on Ollama.
license_note: MiniMax Community License: free for most uses; very large deployments need a separate license.
